title: hemionitis horridula
intro: Hemionitis horridula, also known as the Rough Horsetail Fern, is a species of vascular plant belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Hemionitis horridula include its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic agent. Hemionitis horridula can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
